name: Hainanese Chicken Rice
tip: Ask for less rice and skip the skin to cut ~120 kcal. Extra cucumber adds volume for free.

name: Char Kway Teow
tip: Most calories come from lard and dark soy. Ordering 'less oil' can save ~80 kcal.

name: Laksa
tip: The coconut-milk gravy is calorie-dense — leave some behind to save ~100 kcal.

name: Nasi Lemak
tip: Coconut rice is the main calorie driver. Choose a boiled egg over fried chicken to save ~150 kcal.

name: Roti Prata (Plain)
tip: Pair with dhal instead of sugar curry for extra protein and lower glycemic impact.

name: Roti Prata (Egg)
tip: Egg prata adds ~7g protein for only 40 extra kcal versus plain — a good trade.

name: Chicken Satay
tip: 5 sticks is a reasonable portion. Go easy on the peanut sauce — it adds ~60 kcal per tablespoon.

name: Beef Satay
tip: Beef satay is higher in saturated fat than chicken. Great protein source — watch the sauce portion.

name: Mee Goreng
tip: One of the higher-sodium hawker dishes. Request less oil and sauce to reduce both fat and sodium.

name: Hokkien Mee
tip: Prawn stock base is flavourful but salty. Squeeze the lime wedge — it brightens flavour without extra calories.

name: Bak Kut Teh
tip: Excellent protein-to-calorie ratio. Sodium is very high — limit rice top-ups and soy sauce add-ons.

name: Carrot Cake (White)
tip: White version is lower in sugar than black. Ask for less egg to trim ~50 kcal.

name: Carrot Cake (Black)
tip: Black version gets its colour from sweet dark sauce — adds ~50 kcal over white. Occasional treat is fine.

name: Wonton Mee (Dry)
tip: Dry wonton mee sauce is salty and sweet — ask for it on the side to control intake.

name: Wonton Mee (Soup)
tip: Soup version is ~80 kcal lighter than dry. Avoid drinking all the broth to reduce sodium.

name: Prawn Mee (Soup)
tip: The prawn-pork broth is extremely sodium-rich. Don't drink the soup if you're watching sodium.

name: Prawn Mee (Dry)
tip: Dry version is higher in fat than soup. The prawn paste (hae ko) is calorie-dense — use sparingly.

name: Fried Rice
tip: Wok hei comes from high heat and oil. Ask for less oil and extra egg for a better protein-to-fat ratio.

name: Nasi Biryani (Chicken)
tip: One of the most calorie-dense hawker meals. Share the rice portion or eat half to stay under 400 kcal.

name: Mee Rebus
tip: Sweet-potato-based gravy is thick and starchy. Extra hard-boiled egg adds protein without much fat.

name: Mee Siam
tip: Tangy tamarind gravy is relatively light. Skip the coconut milk drizzle to save ~40 kcal.

name: Popiah (Fresh)
tip: One of the lightest hawker options. Have two rolls for a satisfying meal under 310 kcal.

name: Rojak
tip: Shrimp paste sauce is salty and sweet. The crunchy you tiao puffs are the main fat source — go easy.

name: Yong Tau Foo (Soup)
tip: Choose tofu and veggie items over fried items to lower fat. Clear soup base beats curry or tom yum.

name: Duck Rice
tip: Duck is higher in fat than chicken. Remove the skin to save ~60 kcal without losing much flavour.

name: Roast Pork Rice
tip: Sio bak skin is high in fat — a small portion goes a long way. Pair with blanched greens.

name: Char Siu Rice
tip: The sweet glaze adds sugar calories. Ask for the sauce on the side and control how much you drizzle.

name: Fish Head Curry
tip: Great protein source. Coconut milk in the curry adds saturated fat — eat the fish, leave some gravy.

name: Curry Chicken
tip: Removing skin and eating potato over rice keeps this meal balanced. Coconut milk is calorie-dense.

name: Fried Chicken Wing
tip: Two wings is a common serving. The crispy skin doubles the fat — strip it to halve the fat intake.

name: Fishball Noodle Soup
tip: One of the lighter noodle options. Add extra fishcakes for protein without heavy fat increase.

name: Beef Kway Teow Soup
tip: Tendon and tripe add collagen and flavour with minimal calories. Avoid drinking the full broth.

name: Kway Chap
tip: Intestines and pork belly are high in fat. Choose tofu and lean cuts to keep this below 450 kcal.

name: Oyster Omelette (Orh Luak)
tip: Heavy lard and starch base. Oysters are nutritious but the cooking method adds significant fat.

name: Economy Rice (3 dishes)
tip: Choose 1 protein + 2 vegetables to balance macros. Avoid deep-fried items and gravy-heavy dishes.

name: Lor Mee
tip: The starchy braised gravy is very high in sodium. Request less gravy to cut sodium by ~400mg.

name: Bak Chor Mee (Dry)
tip: Vinegar-based dry version is tangy and lower in fat than many noodle dishes. Add less chilli oil.

name: Bak Chor Mee (Soup)
tip: Soup version is ~80 kcal lighter. The clear broth is sodium-heavy — enjoy but don't drink it all.

name: Claypot Rice
tip: The crispy bottom rice (crust) is delicious but calorie-dense. Share with a friend to halve the intake.

name: Chicken Porridge
tip: One of the lowest-calorie hot meals at the hawker. Add a soft-boiled egg for extra protein.

name: Seafood Fried Rice
tip: Seafood adds lean protein but the wok oil keeps calories high. Ask for less oil where possible.

name: Fried Bee Hoon
tip: Rice vermicelli absorbs a lot of oil. Add a side of tofu or egg for protein balance.

name: Vegetarian Bee Hoon
tip: Great lighter option. Add tau pok (tofu puff) for extra protein and satisfying texture.

name: Thunder Tea Rice (Lei Cha)
tip: Nutrient-dense with lots of vegetables and tofu. Relatively low sodium — one of the healthier hawker bowls.

name: Chilli Crab
tip: Excellent protein source. The egg-thickened sauce is calorie-dense — enjoy the mantou in moderation.

name: Sweet & Sour Pork
tip: Batter coating adds starch and oil. Request less sauce to reduce sugar and sodium.

name: Steamed Fish (Ginger & Soy)
tip: Excellent lean protein with minimal carbs. One of the best macro profiles at the hawker centre.

name: Kaya Toast Set
tip: The slab of cold butter doubles the fat in kaya toast. Kaya alone is lower-cal and naturally sweet.

name: Teh Tarik
tip: Most calories from condensed milk. Ordering 'siu dai' (less sweet) saves ~30 kcal per cup.

name: Kopi
tip: Order 'kopi-o' (black with sugar) for ~50 kcal, or 'kopi-o kosong' for near-zero calories.

name: Milo Dinosaur
tip: The heap of undissolved Milo powder on top is mostly sugar. Stir it in slowly or ask for half the powder.

name: Ice Kachang
tip: Almost all calories from syrups and red bean. Go easy on extra toppings and syrup drizzles.

name: Chendol
tip: Coconut milk and gula melaka are calorie-dense. A smaller bowl still satisfies the craving for ~200 kcal.

name: Goreng Pisang (Banana Fritter)
tip: Batter absorbs significant oil. One piece (~70g) is about 115 kcal — a reasonable snack portion.

name: Muah Chee
tip: Peanut coating adds good fat and protein. Glutinous rice base is dense — enjoy as an occasional snack.

name: Tang Yuan
tip: Sesame or peanut filling adds healthy fats. The sweet ginger soup base is low-cal and warming.

name: Barley Water
tip: One of the lowest-calorie drinks at the hawker. Naturally cooling and good for hydration.

name: Sugarcane Juice
tip: All calories from natural sugars. No added preservatives — but it is still a sugar drink, so enjoy in moderation.

name: Indian Rojak
tip: Fried fritters are the main calorie source. Choose more tofu and boiled items over fried ones.

name: Braised Duck Rice
tip: Braising adds flavour without extra oil but the dark soy sauce is sodium-heavy. Go light on the gravy.
